dashboard.label: T1572 — Protocol Tunneling (chisel)
dashboard.description: Long-lived high-throughput HTTP/S flow detection. Per Zeek conn.log record on a web port, flags multi-minute duration and multi-megabyte bidirectional throughput.

search.query: `protocol_tunnel_chisel` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | stats sum(total_bytes) AS bytes | eval bytes_human = round(bytes/1024/1024, 1) . " MB" | table bytes_human

`protocol_tunnel_chisel` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | eval start=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") | eval duration_min=round(duration/60, 1) | eval orig_mb=round(orig_bytes/1024/1024, 1) | eval resp_mb=round(resp_bytes/1024/1024, 1) | sort -total_bytes | table start, src, dest, dest_port, duration_min, orig_mb, resp_mb, service

index=zeek sourcetype=zeek:conn | where 'id.resp_p' IN (80, 443, 8080, 8443) | where duration >= 60 | eval bucket=case(duration<300, "1-5 min", duration<600, "5-10 min", duration<1800, "10-30 min", duration<3600, "30-60 min", true(), "1h+") | stats count BY bucket | sort bucket

html.p: chisel and similar tunnels (gost, wstunnel) pipe arbitrary TCP traffic through a single long-lived HTTP/HTTPS WebSocket. The server lives on :80 or :443 so egress firewalls treat it like normal web traffic; the implant pipes interactive shells, port forwards, or SOCKS through that one flow.

The detection per-record on conn.log requires all of: a standard HTTP/HTTPS port, duration ≥ 600 s, and combined bidirectional bytes ≥ 10 MB. Streaming media and large downloads can still trigger — populate tunnel_allowlist.csv for known sources.
